Skip to contents
jstats 0.9.102
  • Reference

Internal: convert SAS-style missing values to Stata-style in a data frame

dot-jst_lowercase_tagged_na_df.Rd

Walks the columns of data, converting any SAS-style missing values (.A, .B, ..., stored as haven::tagged_na("A") etc.) to their Stata-style equivalents (.a, .b, ...) in both cell values and the val_labels attribute. haven's write_dta() errors on SAS-style markers in either location (Stata's format is lowercase-only), so this conversion runs unconditionally in jsave()'s .dta branch before write_dta is called.

Usage

.jst_lowercase_tagged_na_df(data)

Arguments

data

A data frame.

Value

List with two elements: data (the data frame with SAS-style markers converted to Stata-style) and n_changed (integer count of columns touched).

Details

A column is counted in n_changed when any SAS-style marker was converted – in cells, in val_labels, or both. Columns already in Stata-style form pass through unchanged and are not counted. Non-double columns are skipped (Stata-style missing values exist only on doubles).

On this page
